title: Small sequence model over recent driver laps, rank-blended with the tree model
summary: Recent F1 research on tyre-energy prediction reports strong performance from deep time-series models, while classic motorsport strategy work also uses neural models for pit decisions.
rationale: Recent F1 research on tyre-energy prediction reports strong performance from deep time-series models, while classic motorsport strategy work also uses neural models for pit decisions. The current pipeline is heavily tabular; a compact GRU/TCN/Transformer over the last k laps per driver-race could capture temporal motifs like steady management versus sudden collapse that hand-built lag features miss.
implementation_hint: Construct fixed-length histories per Year-Race-Driver using only past laps and current-lap covariates. Train a lightweight sequence classifier to predict next-lap pit probability, then blend its OOF ranks with the best CatBoost/GBDT model. Keep architecture small and compare raw probability blend versus rank-average blend for AUC stability.
expected_effect: Primarily diversity gain rather than standalone superiority; the best case is a small but real ensemble boost if temporal patterns differ from tree-learned interactions.
risk: Higher engineering and validation complexity. With only ~439k rows and many short sequences, a deep model can overfit unless masking, padding, and race-grouped OOF evaluation are handled carefully.
